description:
Background:
    As part of a periodic compliance review, you – the Compliance Officer – must show how many successful login events each individual user generated during a specific one-hour window.  
    A small text log has already been exported from the SIEM and placed on the workstation.  
    Your job is to calculate per-user login counts with classic GNU text utilities (no programming language interpreters) and save the results in a CSV file that an automated checker can read.

    Existing material on disk
    1. Log directory:  /home/user/audit/logs/
    2. Raw log file:   /home/user/audit/logs/access.log

       Each line in access.log is in this fixed 4-field format (space-separated):
          YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS <username> login success

       Example (actual data are already present in the file):
          2023-10-01 12:00:01 userA login success

    Required end-state
    1. Create a new directory /home/user/audit/reports/ if it does not yet exist.
    2. Inside that directory, create a CSV file named user_login_frequency.csv
       • The file must be exactly comma-separated, **no extra spaces**.
       • The first line is the header:  username,count
       • Subsequent lines list every username that appears in access.log, followed by that
         user’s total number of “login success” entries.
       • Sort order:  
         a. Primary – descending by the numeric count (highest first).  
         b. Secondary – ascending alphabetically by the username for ties.
    3. Make sure there is a final trailing newline character at the end of the file (the
       automated test will look for it).

    Verification expectations
    • The CSV must contain exactly the usernames found in access.log with their correct counts.  
    • Column separator must be a literal comma “,”.  
    • The file path, name, header text, sort order and trailing newline must match precisely.

    Hint (do **not** paste directly):
    • The task can be completed with a pipeline of 2–3 commands involving sort and uniq.
truth:
Initial file already present before agent starts
------------------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/audit/logs/access.log
Contents (12 lines, each ending with \n):

2023-10-01 12:00:01 userA login success
2023-10-01 12:00:05 userB login success
2023-10-01 12:02:11 userA login success
2023-10-01 12:05:41 userC login success
2023-10-01 12:15:00 userA login success
2023-10-01 12:20:22 userB login success
2023-10-01 12:30:33 userD login success
2023-10-01 12:45:54 userB login success
2023-10-01 12:50:01 userE login success
2023-10-01 13:00:11 userC login success
2023-10-01 13:05:19 userB login success
2023-10-01 13:15:45 userB login success

Expected artefact after successful completion
---------------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/audit/reports/user_login_frequency.csv
Exact contents (including trailing newline):

username,count
userB,5
userA,3
userC,2
userD,1
userE,1
name: task_000000_997944e5
